name: Gyoza a La Ruth
recipeIngredient: 8 Leaves of Napa cabbage, finely chopped
2 Scallions, white only (minced), or dried green onions
1 tbsp Paul Mason Raspberry Cream (or sherry but I prefer this)
1 package Gyoza or potsticker wrappers (round ones)
2 slices Fresh peeled ginger, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 5 cups of water to a boil and dissolve the chicken bouillon cubes.
2. In a bowl, mix ground pork, chopped Napa cabbage, onions, minced ginger, parsley, salt, pepper, sugar, cornstarch, soy sauce, and Raspberry Cream (or sherry).
3. Place a spoonful of filling in the center of each wrapper.
4. Brush 180 degrees of the wrapper lightly with water and fold to close the potsticker, pinching the edges firmly.
5. Heat 1/2 tablespoon peanut oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
6. Arrange the gyoza in the pan so they barely touch each other.
7. Brown on one side only until it forms a golden brown crust.
8. Add 1/4 cup chicken stock and bring to a boil. Cover, reduce to a slow boil, and steam for 8 minutes.
9. Remove the cover and cook over medium heat until all the liquid is absorbed and fully cooked.
10. Repeat steps 5-9 until all the gyozas are cooked.
